strategy: Repair only the missing color-to-winner mapping. Preserve the already grounded conclusion that Sheridan won from the interview transcript, then gather direct visual evidence of Sheridan's jersey color from the game/celebration footage so the trace can map the winner to option B or C.

steps.purpose: Localize clips that show the interviewed Sheridan girls basketball team playing or celebrating, where jersey color is visible.

arguments.query: Sheridan girls basketball team associated with coach Ryan Sullivan playing in the game or celebrating after the win, with team jersey color clearly visible

steps.purpose: Retrieve the most relevant frames from the localized clips where Sheridan players or the winning team are clearly visible in uniform.

arguments.query: frames showing the Sheridan team or winning team in uniform with jersey color clearly visible
time_hints: players in uniform near coach or trophy
game action with Sheridan players visible

steps.purpose: Determine which jersey color belongs to Sheridan/the winning team using the retrieved frames plus the already grounded transcript context that Sheridan won.

arguments.query: Using these frames, identify whether the interviewed Sheridan girls basketball team, which won the game according to the transcript, is wearing white jerseys or black jerseys. Answer only from visible evidence in the frames and note if the color remains unclear.
text_contexts: Pre-grounded transcript context: Ryan Sullivan is identified on screen as Sheridan Girls Basketball Coach. Interview statements indicate Sheridan won, including 'Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most,' 'It was our defense that carried us through the game,' and 'we had to beat really good teams to win... the kids stepped up and did that.'

refinement_instructions: Preserve the supported prior claims that this is a postgame interview with Ryan Sullivan, Sheridan Girls Basketball Coach, and that the transcript supports Sheridan won the game; also preserve that cancellation, tie, and disqualification are not supported. Remove the unsupported prior stopping point that the answer must remain blank solely because jersey-color mapping was not yet grounded. The new evidence is meant to resolve exactly one missing field: whether the winning Sheridan team is the white team or the black team. In the revised trace, use the transcript only to establish that Sheridan won, and use the new visual evidence only to establish Sheridan's jersey color. Then map to the multiple-choice option accordingly. If the new frames still do not clearly tie Sheridan/the winners to white or black uniforms, explicitly state that the color mapping remains unresolved rather than inventing it.
